channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Court rebuffs plea from domestic workers for better pay and respect
item.description: They're often paid low wages and lack job protections. A petition to the country's supreme court to support their demands did not see success — and th...

item.title: 'Part of something that's bigger than you': GoodGym joins volunteering and exercise
item.description: GoodGym, based in the UK, combines exercising with volunteering. How does this combination positively impact people?

item.title: Propofol is associated with lower mortality in sepsis-associated encephalopathy
item.description: 1. Among patients with sepsis-associated encephalopathy, propofol monotherapy was associated with lower one-year all-cause mortality compared to no se...

item.title: How to hone your 'friendship intuition'
item.description: Friendship expert Kat Vellos shares tips on how to make a new friendship stick, including what to do together, how often to hang out — and what to do ...

item.title: Using GLP-1s to maintain a normal weight? There are benefits and risks
item.description: Drugs like Zepbound and Wegovy are intended for people who are overweight. Some patients are using them after bariatric surgery to keep pounds from cr...

item.title: Olympic athletes push their bodies to the limit. Should we?
item.description: Elite athletes often push through pain to achieve victory. But, everyday exercisers need to distinguish between soreness which is normal and pain whic...

item.title: Exposure to intense wildfire smoke during pregnancy may be linked to increased likelihood of autism
item.description: New research suggests that exposure to intense wildfire smoke during pregnancy may be associated with an increased likelihood of autism in children. T...

item.title: US FDA Approves Labeling Changes to Menopause Hormone Therapies
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration said on Thursday it has approved ⁠labeling changes to six menopause hormone therapies to remove references to ri...

item.title: Connected health care workflows: From chore to core patient care
item.description: Today’s clinicians are spending more time documenting care than delivering it. This reality is not lost on health care providers, many of whom feel bu...

item.title: Commonly used drugs could help prevent delirium after surgery
item.description: A new study has found that several commonly used drugs could significantly reduce the risk of delirium in older people following surgery. Delirium—a s...

item.title: 'Astounding' Number of Americans Microdosing Psychedelics
item.description: About 10 million US adults microdosed psilocybin, LSD, or MDMA in 2025, a new probability-based, nationally representative survey shows. Medscape Medi...

item.title: Nearly 38% of Cancers Potentially Avoidable: Global Study
item.description: Three major modifiable risk factors account for much of the attributable cancer burden, but substantial variations across regions and between sexes ex...

item.title: Immigrant caregiver burden: the hidden cost of the five-year Medicaid wait
item.description: Every night I spend at home, I wake up to the sound of Für Elise by Ludwig van Beethoven. If you asked my dad, who works remotely, he would grimace an...

item.title: What to know about common Winter Olympics injuries
item.description: As the world collectively watches the 2026 Winter Olympics, we are in awe of athletes making their intense sports look graceful and natural. What we d...

item.title: Nonablative Radiofrequency Improves Menopause Sexual Health
item.description: Nonablative capacitive-resistive monopolar radiofrequency therapy improved sexual function and vaginal health in postmenopausal women with genitourina...

item.title: Mindfulness Boosts Tolerance of Awake Endoscopy
item.description: Teaching breathing and relaxation techniques before awake endoscopy improved tolerance and supported early cancer detection in outpatients. Medscape N...

item.title: Researchers identify promising Alzheimer's disease drug target
item.description: Indiana University School of Medicine scientists have identified a promising drug target for Alzheimer's disease. The team found that removing an enzy...

item.title: As nights warm, study flags possible prenatal link to autism risk
item.description: Higher nighttime temperatures during pregnancy may be associated with a higher risk of autism diagnosis in children, according to a new study led by r...

item.title: Business literacy empowers physicians to lead sustainable health systems [PODCAST]
item.description: Subscribe to The Podcast by KevinMD. Watch on YouTube. Catch up on old episodes! Family physician Kelly Bain discusses her article “Why physician busi...

item.title: The Scan by 2 Minute Medicine®: Daily caffeine intake linked to lower dementia risk, AAP sues over federal vaccine schedule overhaul, Catherine O’Hara passes away after short illness, and GLP-1 receptor agonists reduce endometrial cancer risk
item.description: The Scan by 2 Minute Medicine® is a pop-culture medical newsletter and exclusive benefit for 2 Minute Medicine Plus subscribers. Moderate caffeine int...

item.title: Polypharmacy is associated with higher risk of mortality and hospitalizations among older adults
item.description: 1. Polypharmacy was associated with a higher risk of mortality and hospitalization at one-year and five-years among older adults aged >75 years. Evide...

item.title: The necessity of getting lost to find yourself
item.description: We are born into corridors, measured, labeled, painted neutral, walls rising before we learn how wide our thoughts can stretch. Lines are drawn early:...

item.title: Physician resilience: Why systems matter more than heroism
item.description: Medicine has always loved the story of the hero. The physician who pushes through exhaustion. The one who catches the rare diagnosis at the last secon...

item.title: Oral nonstatin medication significantly lowers cholesterol in high-risk patients
item.description: 1. In this randomized controlled trial, once-daily oral enlicitide was associated with sustained reductions in low-density lipoprotein cholesterol lev...

item.title: Status epilepticus cause may predict likelihood of neurologic recovery but not short-term mortality
item.description: 1. The underlying cause of status epilepticus is strongly associated with the likelihood of neurologic recovery, but does not independently predict sh...
